definition: AI-related capital expenditure flows of $40-60B/month documented in TIC data provide real dollar support. The capex dollar is structurally distinct from carry/positioning flows — it reverses only when the investment thesis deteriorates, not on risk sentiment shifts. However, the structural break framing is not supported, as the dot-com parallel (1997-2001) ended in one of the sharpest dollar corrections in modern history.
evidence: Capex dollar distinction praised in pair_0 as analytically precise
Dot-com parallel cited as support ended in sharp dollar correction
AI capex premium estimate 3-12 cents EUR/USD is too wide to be actionable
Flow persistence is a judgment call, not a data point (challenger_01 own words)
open_questions: How long will AI capex flows persist at current levels?
What would cause the AI investment thesis to deteriorate?
Is the 3-12 cent EUR/USD premium narrowable with better data?
How much of AI capex is truly incremental vs. redirected from other investment?
